data-reader/pom.xml.general_idea: Switch from separate source/target configuration to the unified release flag for Java 21.
data-reader/pom.xml.why_exists: The 'source' and 'target' options are deprecated in favor of 'release' in newer compiler plugin versions; using 'release' ensures consistent compilation for the target JDK.

tetrad-gui/src/main/java/edu/cmu/tetrada.general_idea: Replace deprecated Class.newInstance() with Constructor.newInstance() to comply with Java 17+ deprecation and Java 21 strictness.
tetrad-gui/src/main/java/edu/cmu/tetrada.why_exists: Class.newInstance() is deprecated for removal in Java 17 and may be removed in future JDKs; it also fails to propagate checked exceptions from the constructor, leading to runtime errors that are harder to debug. While it might still compile in Java 21 depending on the specific build configuration, it is a breaking change in terms of API stability and best practices enforced by modern tooling.

While it might still compile in Java 21 depending on the specific build configuration, it is a breaking change in terms of API stability and best practices enforced by modern tooling.

tetrad-gui/src/main/java/edu/cmu/tetrada.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
tetrad-gui/src/main/java/edu/cmu/tetrada.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct semantic marking of serialization fields in modern Java.

tetrad-gui/src/main/java/edu/cmu/tetrada.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
tetrad-gui/src/main/java/edu/cmu/tetrada.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is defined in java.io.Serial; without the import, the code will fail to compile.

tetrad-gui/src/main/java/edu/cmu/tetrada.general_idea: Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() for accessing the first element.
tetrad-gui/src/main/java/edu/cmu/tetrada.why_exists: List.get(0) is deprecated for removal in Java 21; using it causes deprecation warnings and will fail to compile in future JDKs.
